title: Schoenus laticulmis
taxonomy: Schoenus laticulmis is classified within the family Cyperaceae. This family consists of sedge plants, which are commonly found in wetland environments. Schoenus laticulmis is further classified into the genus Schoenus, which is a group of sedges native to Australia. The species Schoenus laticulmis is a type of tussock sedge, characterized by its distinctive growth habit and leaf morphology. Schoenus laticulmis is also classified as a flowering plant within the division Magnoliophyta.

intro: Schoenus laticulmis, commonly known as the broad-leaved bog-rush, is a perennial, rhizomatous sedge plant native to Australia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for wound healing and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Schoenus laticulmis include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
